name: Pet insurance
summary: Accident and illness insurance for dogs and cats, $15-$150/month depending on breed, age, coverage tier.

evidence.claim: NAIC Pet Insurance Model Act adopted 2022 standardizing definitions of pre-existing condition, waiting period, and disclosure requirements. Adopted by 17+ states as of 2025.

North American Pet Health Insurance Association (NAPHIA) 2024 State of the Industry Report: average accident+illness premium $676/yr dogs, $383/yr cats; typical claim reimbursement 70-90% after deductible.
FTC 2019 staff report on pet insurance deceptive practices identifying common exclusions (hip dysplasia, breed-specific conditions, behavioral issues) that consumers commonly miss.

criteria.name: annual_coverage_cap
criteria.notes: Unlimited coverage is highest tier; $10-15k is common mid-tier.

criteria.name: covers_dental_illness
criteria.notes: Frequently excluded or gated by dental cleaning requirement.

criteria.name: covers_behavioral
criteria.notes: Frequently excluded — NAIC Model Act requires disclosure (E1).

criteria.name: covers_hereditary
criteria.notes: Breed-specific hereditary conditions (hip dysplasia, BOAS) commonly excluded (E3).

criteria.name: age_cap
criteria.notes: Some policies drop senior pets at 10-14yr; NAIC Model Act requires disclosure (E1).

confabulationPatterns.reality: No pet policy covers pre-existing conditions, and breed-specific hereditary conditions (hip dysplasia in large breeds, BOAS in brachycephalic breeds) are commonly excluded even on top-tier plans (E3). NAIC Model Act requires this disclosure in states that adopted it (E1).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the policy exclude pre-existing conditions? Which breed-specific conditions are excluded?

confabulationPatterns.pattern: lower premium now, same coverage forever
confabulationPatterns.reality: Premiums rise with pet age, typically 8-15%/year. A puppy policy at $25/mo often reaches $80+/mo by age 10. Underwrite the projected lifetime cost, not the introductory price.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the pricing quoted for the first year only, or is the projected 10-year lifetime cost disclosed?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Accident waiting period is typically 2-14 days, illness 14-30 days, hereditary conditions 6-12 months. 'Instant coverage' refers only to enrollment, not claim eligibility.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What is the waiting period for accidents, illness, and hereditary conditions?

compatibilityQuestions: Breed? Large dogs and brachycephalic breeds (Bulldogs, Pugs) face highest hereditary exclusions.
Pet's current age? Enrolling before age 2 minimizes pre-existing exclusions.
Do you have a savings cushion? For healthy pets, self-insuring with a dedicated savings account often beats insurance TCO over 10 years.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Dental cleaning requirement to maintain coverage

representativeSkus.name: Dog Accident + Illness
representativeSkus.notes: Unlimited coverage; pays vet directly in most cases.

representativeSkus.name: Complete Coverage Dog
representativeSkus.notes: Best behavioral + dental coverage in class.

representativeSkus.name: Pet Base Plan Dog
representativeSkus.notes: Lowest premium but multiple exclusions; check fine print.
